turningPoints.play: Robinson 3' Cutting Layup Shot (10 PTS) (Adebayo 3 AST)

Game Drama Score.details: Decided by 3 points with 4 lead changes and 14 ties. The game saw 17 major win probability swings, and the losing team's chances peaked at 92%.

Most Valuable Play.details: Q4, 10:10, Vincent (MIA): Vincent 26' 3PT Jump Shot (21 PTS) (Adebayo 2 AST). It swung the win probability by +21.7 points.

Most Damaging Play.details: Q3, 2:51, Zeller (MIA): Zeller 4' Layup (4 PTS) (Butler 8 AST). It swung the win probability by -13.1 points.

Clutch-Time Scoring Summary.value: Scoring over the final five minutes: home team 15, away team 10.

recap.Biggest Turning Point: The game turned at Q4, 10:10: Vincent 26' 3PT Jump Shot (21 PTS) (Adebayo 2 AST). That single play moved DEN's chance of winning from 70% to 45%. No other moment shifted the game more, and it went MIA's way.
recap.Player Impact: No one influenced the outcome more than Jokić of DEN. Across 56 plays, his actions moved the win probability by a combined 171 percentage points, the most of any player on the floor.
recap.Comeback Reality: The tensest moment for a comeback came at Q4, 0:48.3, with DEN down 5 points. The model put their chance of pulling it off at 12%. In other words: difficult, but not nothing.
recap.Hidden Momentum: The strongest run that didn't fully show on the scoreboard came at Q2, 9:57, with the game tilting DEN's way. The play in the middle of it: Murray 24' 3PT Running Jump Shot (3 PTS) (Braun 2 AST).
recap.Model Note: These numbers come from ClutchCast's champion model, the winner of a competition between six models. The winner is judged on how honest its probabilities are on games it never trained on, not just how often it picks the right winner.
